mechanism_thesis: context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index) HURTS on these branches: the extra context dimension inflates the coverage map so naive_ctx hoards context-redundant corpus entries and spreads its mutation budget thin, while naive's flat per-edge map stays focused and resolves. naive_ctx BLOCKS, naive RESOLVES.

hypotheses.label: ctx inflates the corpus / map so naive_ctx hoards far more (context-redundant) entries than naive

params.note: count of saved corpus entries per trial; context-redundant hoarding => naive_ctx >> naive

hypotheses.prediction: corpus_count_ratio >= 1.5 (naive_ctx hoards >=1.5x the corpus naive keeps, because the same edges count as new under each call-context). A NON-ctx penalty (e.g. naive simply got luckier on byte mutation) would predict corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0 -- no systematic corpus bloat on the blocking arm.
hypotheses.scope: corpus_queue exists ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (data_realities). Of the 6 members, ONLY harfbuzz_5802 and harfbuzz_6124_h1 are scorable; lcms/libpng/libxml2 fall through to the fallback.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out corpus_count_ratio ~ 1.0; rules out 'naive just won by chance' alternative
hypotheses.g2_independent: measures corpus SIZE (the inflation mechanism), independent of the resolve pattern (which gate side each fuzzer takes) that defined the shape

hypotheses.label: a high-call-count / multi-context path at the gate is what ctx fragments (flooding) vs a shallow exact-literal gate (dilution)

params.source: per-role coverage call counts at the blocker function (e.g. png_read_IDAT_data 2750@L vs 136@W)

hypotheses.prediction: FLOODING members (deep high-iteration loop, e.g. libpng inflate loop) show call_count_lift >> 1 (naive_ctx re-enters the loop function far more, manufacturing the (ctx,edge) flood); DILUTION members (shallow 1-hop exact-literal gate, e.g. harfbuzz_6124) show call_count_lift ~ 1 -- the gate is shallow, the penalty is pure signal-splitting not loop flooding. An 'all members are the same flooding mechanism' alternative would predict call_count_lift >> 1 uniformly.
hypotheses.scope: needs per-role / coverage call-count data at the blocker function for both arms; the cards expose this for libpng (2750 vs 136) and harfbuzz, partial elsewhere.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: could come out uniform; separates flooding from dilution
hypotheses.g2_independent: call-count at the gate function is a structural property of the path, not the resolve pattern

decidable_caveat: PARTIALLY decidable. The corpus_inflation test (the primary ctx-anti verification) is scorable on only 2 of 6 members (harfbuzz_5802, harfbuzz_6124_h1) because on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (data_realities). lcms/libpng/libxml2 have NO corpus and fall to fallback:inconclusive. The depth_inflation test that would SEPARATE flooding (libpng) from dilution (libxml2/harfbuzz) leans on per-role call-count data that is fully present only for the cards' cited examples. Per G3, members the data cannot discriminate are carried by fallback:inconclusive rather than asserted.
notes: MERGES: the three prior clusters (ctx_valid_path_corpus_anchoring, ctx_loop_call_context_flooding, ctx_exact_literal_signal_dilution) are ONE technique-effect at the verification level -- ctx map inflation that dilutes/starves the focused signal naive keeps. They differ in the gate they sit on (length-underflow anchoring @ lcms; deep-loop flooding @ libpng; exact-literal dilution @ libxml2/harfbuzz) but all predict the SAME primary signature: naive_ctx hoards a context-redundant corpus and loses the focused byte-mutation race. I collapsed them into ctx_corpus_inflation (the discriminable, verifiable hypothesis) and kept ctx_depth_inflation_at_gate ONLY as the one axis a measurement could still separate (flooding's deep loop vs dilution's shallow gate, via call_count_lift). TARGETS: corpus-scorable members are harfbuzz_5802, harfbuzz_6124_h1 only. data_realities respected: (a) NO seed_lineage/event measurement -- ctx, like VP, is a FEEDBACK invisible in mutation_op, so the only handle is what the corpus REACHES/HOARDS (size, call-depth), never a lineage 'ctx event'; (b) corpus_size_ratio scored only on the 4 corpus targets; (c) no byte-alignment claim (gates here are mixed length/literal/structural). CAVEAT: ctx_valid_path_corpus_anchoring (lcms_2149) is the weakest merge -- its mechanism is direction-INVERTED (ctx keeps inputs LARGE, naive SHRINKS), so corpus_count_ratio>=1.5 may hold but for a size reason; lcms has no corpus and is INCONCLUSIVE regardless. If corpus_inflation is judged purely a corpus-scale artifact non-discriminable from generic ctx overhead on the non-corpus targets, the whole shape collapses to G3 inconclusive for lcms/libpng/libxml2; only the two harfbuzz members can carry an evidence-confirmed ctx-anti label.
